news.title: STAT+: At AACR, a provocative use of CAR-T, Merck’s new thing and cancer’s geography problem
news.summary: At AACR, a bold use of CAR-T raises hopes and provocative questions about its use in smoldering multiple myeloma.

news.title: STAT+: Former banker brought on to revitalize Pfizer’s strategy departs his post
news.summary: Andrew Baum, a former Citi analyst, has left his role as Pfizer's executive vice president and chief strategy and innovation officer.

news.title: STAT+: In early trial, CAR-T results raise hope of preventing multiple myeloma in high-risk patients
news.summary: A CAR-T trial shows promise in preventing smoldering multiple myeloma from becoming the real thing.

news.title: STAT+: Trump order to advance psychedelic treatments generates excitement — and worries
news.summary: President Trump’s executive order on was largely applauded by advocates. But some also quietly worried the White House’s role risks politicizing their field and undermining its credibility.

news.title: Opinion: The contradiction at the heart of Republicans’ embrace of psychedelics
news.summary: “The research is clear about one thing: Recovery is not primarily a pharmacological event,” write Dimitri Mugianis and Ross Ellenhorn.

news.title: STAT+: Eli Lilly’s $3.25 billion acquisition of Kelonia Therapeutics caps startup’s tortuous ride
news.summary: Kelonia Therapeutics, a small biotech company, long struggled to stay afloat. Now it's been acquired by Eli Lilly for $3.25 billion.

news.title: STAT+: The PBMs fight back, and arbitration doesn’t lose
news.summary: We unpack the contentious fight over drug price transparency.

news.title: STAT+: Finally cracking KRAS as a druggable target
news.summary: The Breakthrough Prize winners, Trump's psychedelics executive order, and more biotech news from The Readout

news.title: STAT+: Pharmalittle: We’re reading about Trump boosting psychedelic treatment, the future for weight-loss drugs, and more
news.summary: And more pharma news from the Pharmalot campus via the Pharmalittle newsletter

news.title: How weed affects adolescent brains
news.summary: The latest CDC director nominee, the ongoing measles outbreak, and other health news from Morning Rounds

news.title: STAT+: Extended use of Nektar Therapeutics drug shows promise in alopecia
news.summary: Extended use of a drug from Nektar Therapeutics promoted hair growth in people with alopecia.

news.title: STAT+: A controversy over research monkeys highlights ambiguity over health standards
news.summary: A leading animal rights group is accusing Pfizer of running afoul of its own standards in the handling of research monkeys.

news.title: Opinion: Politicians are using low teen birth rates to further restrict access to birth control, abortion
news.summary: “Both narratives — that teen birth rates are too high or too low — problematize teen pregnancy and childbearing,” writes Riley J. Steiner.

news.title: STAT+: PBMs warn Trump’s proposal to disclose drug prices is illegal
news.summary: Pharma industry middlemen are pushing back on a major drug price transparency proposal.

news.title: Opinion: Measles outbreak means my immunocompromised son can’t leave the house without extreme safety measures
news.summary: Hundreds of thousands of families are making life-or-death decisions as measles continues to spread.

news.title: UnitedHealthcare to eliminate most prior authorizations for rural providers
news.summary: UnitedHealthcare is building on its work to support rural hospitals, and will now exempt these facilities from most prior authorizations.

news.title: CVS, Mass General Brigham primary care deal would increase annual care spending by $40M, report predicts
news.summary: A "conservative" preliminary estimate from a Massachusetts watchdog agency acknowledged likely access gains from a pending MinuteClinic affiliation deal, but said the major health system's higher prices would substantially boost commercial healthcare spending.

news.title: Chutes & Ladders—Blue Shield of California names chief pharmacy officer; ChristianaCare CEO to retire
news.summary: Welcome to this week's Chutes & Ladders, our roundup of hirings, firings and retirings throughout the industry. Please submit the good news—or the bad—from your shop, and we will feature it here at the end of each week.

news.title: A look inside Highmark and Spring Health's mental well-being partnership
news.summary: Just over two years ago, Highmark joined forces with Spring Health to launch a new mental well-being platform that made it far easier for members to access critical services. Now, the partners are offering a look at how that program has worked for members.

news.title: Hippocratic AI rolls out 2 new tools aimed at expanding clinical access, improving nurse workflow
news.summary: The startup developed the tools, dubbed AI Front Door and Nurse Co-Pilot, as voice AI tools for patients and inpatient nurses.

news.title: In Connecticut, doctors now sue patients most over medical bills, surpassing hospitals
news.summary: Records show lawsuits by doctors and other nonhospital providers now account for more than 80% of healthcare collections cases filed against patients and their families in 2024.

news.title: Why Payers Need Agent-to-Agent Orchestration
news.summary: To move beyond fragmented pilot programs and achieve truly efficient service, payers must adopt a strategic, unified approach to managing their intelligent workforce.

news.title: Breast cancer’s next precision medicine revolution may lie in molecular residual disease
news.summary: New data shows potential for ctDNA to guide treatment decisions for patients with triple-negative breast cancer

news.title: Beyond the Visit: How AI Companion Technology Is Reshaping Outcomes for Aging Populations
news.summary: Most care happens between visits, and most of it is invisible. New AI companion models are changing what plans can see, measure, and influence.

news.title: Oscar unveils Lucie, its one-stop shop for individual market plans, supplemental benefits
news.summary: Oscar Health is aiming to rethink the way people shop for coverage with the launch of its new marketplace, Lucie.

news.title: DOJ seeks immediate asset freeze, receivership against telehealth company Zealthy
news.summary: The Department of Justice filed a motion seeking an immediate asset freeze and receivership for telehealth company Zealthy and its CEO, Kyle Robertson, over alleged fraudulent prescribing and other deceptive practices, according to court documents.

news.title: Affordability, transparency: A look at large employers' top healthcare concerns
news.summary: The Purchaser Business Group on Health has released new data that highlights some of the key healthcare priorities of its jumbo employer members, with—perhaps unsurprisingly—the escalating affordability crisis as the biggest challenge.

news.title: AACR: Endeavoring to go global, Zai Lab offers reality check on China’s biotech ascent
news.summary: Since its 2014 founding, Zai Lab has built its business by licensing drugs from Big Pharma. But in the last three years, the company has sought to leverage its footholds in China and the U.S. to become a multinational biopharma in its own right.

news.title: Biogen inks $850M biobucks deal with TJ Biopharma for China rights to late-stage immune antibody
news.summary: Following its move to secure ex-China rights to the CD38-directed antibody felzartamab, Biogen is doubling down and building on an existing partnership with local drugmaker TJ Biopharma.

news.title: Lilly picks up another in vivo CAR-T company with Kelonia buyout worth up to $7B
news.summary: Eli Lilly is picking up its second in vivo CAR-T company of the year, paying $3.25 billion in upfront cash for Kelonia Therapeutics and its phase 1-stage myeloma therapy.

news.title: Roche boosts diagnostics offerings with $595M Saga buyout
news.summary: Roche is handing over $595 million to bolster its Foundation Medicine business with a deal to snap up SAGA Diagnostics.

news.title: Novo readies SCD drug from $1.1B Forma buyout for approval push after phase 3 win
news.summary: Novo Nordisk is gearing up to submit its rare blood disorder drug to the FDA after the former Forma Therapeutics asset reduced a painful complication of sickle cell disease (SCD) in a phase 3 study.

news.title: When Spray Drying Falls Short: A Faster Path Forward
news.summary: Learn how early intervention can help drug developers convert suboptimal formulations into scalable, clinically viable solutions.

news.title: How WuXi AppTec’s Integrated Platform Is Helping Therapies Move Faster from Discovery to Patients
news.summary: In a complex therapeutic landscape, WuXi AppTec is simplifying the path from discovery to patient. Find out how.

news.title: Biomarkers are reshaping oncology trial design
news.summary: Discover how biomarker strategies are transforming oncology trials and accelerating precision medicine breakthroughs.

news.title: 3 bioprocessing trends—and the critical enabler that’s quietly driving them
news.summary: Bioprocessing is evolving rapidly in a shifting landscape of innovative and complex products, processes and technologies. In this new era, three important trends are materializing—and there’s a critical (but perhaps unsung) enabler to thank for that: modernized, single-use systems such as leak-free 2D bags.

news.title: AstraZeneca’s faith in IL-33 inhibitor continues to pay off with latest phase 3 COPD win
news.summary: AstraZeneca’s chronic obstructive pulmonary disease (COPD) candidate has served up a third phase 3 win, offering further evidence the Big Pharma may have cracked the anti-IL-33 code.

news.title: Odyssey charts fresh voyage to public markets after abandoning IPO plans last year
news.summary: In another sign that the biotech IPO market is heating back up, Odyssey Therapeutics has made a fresh announcement of its intention to go public.

news.title: Agenus focuses on survival data as 0% response rate triggers primary miss
news.summary: A phase 2 trial combining Agenus’ and MiNK Therapeutics’ cancer candidates has missed its primary endpoint. But with the study generating early evidence the cocktail improves survival, the biotechs said the data support further assessment of the combination.

news.title: 中国械企十年跨境并购：有人豪掷59亿买巨亏，有人花1亿赚10倍
news.summary: 中资械企跨境并购回暖，逻辑从技术引进转向全球化布局。

papers.title: Geometric coherence of single-cell CRISPR perturbations reveals regulatory architecture and predicts cellular stress
papers.summary: Genome engineering has achieved remarkable sequence-level precision, yet predicting the transcriptomic state that a cell will occupy after perturbation remains an open problem. Single-cell CRISPR screens measure how far cells move from their unperturbed state, but this effect magnitude ignores a fundamental question: do the cells move together? Two perturbations with identical magnitude can produce qualitatively different outcomes if one drives cells coherently along a shared trajectory while the other scatters them across expression space. We introduce a geometric stability metric, Shesha, th

papers.title: Universal statistical signatures of evolution in artificial intelligence architectures
papers.summary: We test whether artificial intelligence architectural evolution obeys the same statistical laws as biological evolution. Compiling 935 ablation experiments from 161 publications, we show that the distribution of fitness effects (DFE) of architectural modifications follows a heavy-tailed Student's t-distribution with proportions (68% deleterious, 19% neutral, 13% beneficial for major ablations, n=568) that place AI between compact viral genomes and simple eukaryotes. The DFE shape matches D. melanogaster (normalized KS=0.07) and S. cerevisiae (KS=0.09); the elevated beneficial fraction (13% vs.

papers.title: TwinTrack: Post-hoc Multi-Rater Calibration for Medical Image Segmentation
papers.summary: Pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma (PDAC) segmentation on contrast-enhanced CT is inherently ambiguous: inter-rater disagreement among experts reflects genuine uncertainty rather than annotation noise. Standard deep learning approaches assume a single ground truth, producing probabilistic outputs that can be poorly calibrated and difficult to interpret under such ambiguity. We present TwinTrack, a framework that addresses this gap through post-hoc calibration of ensemble segmentation probabilities to the empirical mean human response (MHR) -the fraction of expert annotators labeling a voxel as t

papers.title: RadAgent: A tool-using AI agent for stepwise interpretation of chest computed tomography
papers.summary: Vision-language models (VLM) have markedly advanced AI-driven interpretation and reporting of complex medical imaging, such as computed tomography (CT). Yet, existing methods largely relegate clinicians to passive observers of final outputs, offering no interpretable reasoning trace for them to inspect, validate, or refine. To address this, we introduce RadAgent, a tool-using AI agent that generates CT reports through a stepwise and interpretable process. Each resulting report is accompanied by a fully inspectable trace of intermediate decisions and tool interactions, allowing clinicians to ex
